message:
feat(config,runtime): add TLS configuration subsystem and runtime hot-apply engine

Introduce a complete TLS configuration layer and its runtime
integration — the foundation for Redis-compatible encrypted transport:

fr-config (+709 lines):
- TlsProtocol enum: TLSv1.2 / TLSv1.3 with flexible case-insensitive
  parsing (accepts "tlsv1.2", "tls1.2", "tlsv1_2", etc.)
- TlsAuthClients enum: off / optional / required with Redis-compatible
  token mapping ("yes"/"on"/"required" all map to Required)
- TlsConfig struct: full config surface — tls_port, cert/key/ca file
  paths, protocol list, cipher string, auth_clients policy,
  cluster_announce_tls_port, max_new_tls_connections_per_cycle
- TlsRuntimeState: tracks live TLS listener status, active config,
  and connection-type-configured flag for hot-reconfiguration
- TlsListenerTransition / TlsCfgError: transition planner and
  strongly-typed error hierarchy for config validation failures
- plan_tls_runtime_apply(): pure function that computes the minimal
  state transition (enable/disable listener, swap context, configure
  connection type) without side effects
- evaluate_tls_hardened_deviation(): maps TLS config errors to the
  existing hardened-deviation framework for policy-gated error handling

fr-runtime (+173 lines):
- Wire TlsRuntimeState into the Runtime struct with default
  initialization
- apply_tls_config(): orchestrates the full hot-apply lifecycle —
  plans the transition, clones and patches state, records evidence in
  the audit ledger, and handles policy-gated errors through the
  existing threat/deviation framework
- tls_runtime_state() accessor for introspection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
